regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean conditions today are stable with light east winds 7 knots and temperatures near 74°F in San Juan. Expect increasing easterly winds up to 16-18 knots with partly cloudy skies and elevated rain chances (up to 55%) later today, tapering to 15% by Tuesday and under 10% by Wednesday. Mariners should secure loose gear and monitor localized showers, especially near San Juan and St. Thomas, where squalls may reduce visibility. Fishing captains and yacht operators should plan for moderate chop and east swell; anchorage conditions remain generally good but remain vigilant for brief heavier rain bands when approaching ports like St. Maarten or Turks & Caicos.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean Briefing for March 15, 2026: Current conditions in Cozumel show 79°F with southeast winds at 7 knots under overcast skies and no rain expected today. Expect increasing southeasterly winds to 12 knots with isolated rain showers tomorrow, easing to mostly overcast with a 15% rain chance on day two. Day three brings a wind shift to north-northeast at 18 knots and a higher likelihood of rain showers near 80%, which may impact visibility and sea state, especially around Roatan and Belize City anchorages. Captains should monitor changing wind directions for safe maneuvering; cruise passengers advised to carry light rain gear on days two and three and plan outdoor activities accordingly.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region conditions for March 15, 2026 show light ESE winds at 7 knots and overcast skies with minimal rain chance at Nassau. Expect increasing ESE to SE winds through Day 2, peaking near 15 knots with drizzle and rising rain probability up to 38 percent, then shifting to northerly at 15 knots on Day 3 with higher rain chances near 68 percent. Mariners and cruise operators should prepare for reduced visibility and wet decks, especially near Freeport and Bimini where drizzle is expected. Fishing captains should plan for moderate chop and cautious operations in exposed anchorages; small craft advisories are not yet warranted but monitoring is advised as winds increase.
regions.southern-caribbean:
Southern Caribbean weather for March 15, 2026: Aruba currently reports 79°F with east winds 17 knots, partly cloudy skies, and an 18% chance of rain. Expect increasing east winds through the next three days, reaching Small Craft Advisory thresholds on day two with sustained 22 knots. Rain chances peak at 35% today with scattered showers; conditions will clear slightly by day three but remain overcast. Mariners in Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena should prepare for steady easterly trade winds near 15-20 knots, generally stable but with localized showers possible.

Cruise and yacht captains should secure light gear topside and monitor wind shifts, especially near open anchorages off Aruba and Bonaire. Fishing operators can plan early morning trips to avoid afternoon showers and increased chop. Passengers should carry light rain protection and anticipate breezy conditions on decks and shore excursions.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean conditions today feature light east-southeast winds 8-13 knots and partly cloudy skies with a low rain chance near 15-25 percent at ports including Roatan, Belize City, Grand Cayman, Cozumel, and Costa Maya. Expect gradual wind shifts to southeast 11-14 knots over the next two days with isolated showers increasing slightly to 25-28 percent. By day three, thunderstorms become likely with rain chances rising above 80 percent, creating potentially hazardous conditions for small craft, especially near exposed anchorages at Grand Cayman and Belize City. Fishing and yacht captains should plan for sheltered mooring by midweek and monitor updates for possible Small Craft Advisories as winds increase. Passengers should carry rain gear and prepare for localized squalls during afternoon hours.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles ports including Barbados, St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, Antigua, St. Kitts, St. Maarten, St. Vincent, and Grenada will experience unsettled weather today with scattered rain showers and ENE winds sustained near 20 knots. Small Craft Advisories remain in effect across the region due to these steady trade winds and choppy seas. Expect increasing rain coverage in Barbados and eastern islands through the day with up to 80 percent chance of showers, reducing to mostly partly cloudy conditions by March 16 and 17. Mariners should secure loose gear, monitor squalls near open anchorages such as Carlisle Bay (Barbados) and Marigot Bay (St. Lucia), and fishing captains should plan for limited visibility and brief periods of moderate seas during showers. Passengers advised to carry rain gear and exercise caution on wet decks.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles region ports will see mild conditions today with temperatures rising from 60 to near 71 degrees Fahrenheit. Winds remain light from the south-southwest at 4 to 8 knots, with persistent fog reducing visibility especially near Los Angeles / San Pedro and Catalina Island. Mariners should exercise caution when entering or leaving harbor due to limited sight lines. No rain or hazardous weather is expected over the next three days, but overcast skies will develop by day three with southwest winds around 7 to 9 knots. Cruise and fishing vessels can expect calm seas and good operating conditions, but maintain vigilance in fog-prone areas.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada area conditions are stable with light east winds at 1 knot and overcast skies this morning, temperature near 57°F and no precipitation expected. Visibility may be reduced by fog today and tomorrow, especially near Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos, so mariners should exercise caution when navigating in these areas and use radar and AIS to maintain safe separation. Winds will shift to a moderate southwest flow around 7 knots over the next three days, with temperatures rising to the upper 70s and mid-80s by day three; no marine wind warnings are in effect. Cruise operators and fishing captains should plan for light seas and limited rain, but remain alert for fog-related visibility issues during early departures and arrivals.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas and surrounding ports remain calm with clear skies and steady northwest winds at 12 to 16 knots through the next 72 hours. Temperatures will hold in the low to mid 70s Fahrenheit with no rain expected. Mariners should note moderate northwest winds favor good sea conditions for fishing and yacht operations; expect smooth anchorage in Cabo San Lucas and San Jose del Cabo harbors. Cruise passengers can enjoy pleasant daytime temperatures and clear visibility for shore excursions; advise securing light gear on decks due to steady breeze.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan region conditions remain stable with light northwest winds 6-10 knots and no rain expected through March 17. Skies will alternate between clear and overcast, with temperatures steady near 75-76°F. Mariners should expect calm seas and good visibility for safe navigation and anchoring at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata ports. Fishing captains can plan for productive outings with minimal weather disruption, while cruise passengers can enjoy outdoor activities with low heat and no precipitation concerns.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta region is enjoying clear skies with light northeast winds at 2 knots and a temperature near 78 degrees Fahrenheit. Through the next three days, expect mild west to southwest winds between 7 and 8 knots, temperatures holding steady around 79 to 81 degrees, and no rain. Mariners should note calm seas and good visibility, ideal for safe navigation and fishing in Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala anchorages. Cruise passengers can plan outdoor activities comfortably, but yacht and fishing captains should monitor the slight increase in westerly winds in the afternoons for optimal course adjustments.
regions.western-mediterranean:
Western Mediterranean Weather Briefing for March 15, 2026:

Current conditions in Barcelona show clear skies with a light WNW breeze at 7 knots and temperatures around 48°F. Winds will remain moderate over the next three days, shifting slightly westward and holding between 4-8 knots, with partly cloudy skies and no rain expected. Mariners operating near Barcelona, Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, Ibiza, Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar can expect calm seas and good visibility, ideal for cruising, fishing, or yacht anchoring. Passengers should dress in layers due to cool mornings warming into the mid-60s, and captains should maintain routine checks but can expect no significant weather hazards or advisories during this period.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean weather for March 15, 2026, shows generally mild conditions with overcast skies and light northeast to east winds 4-12 knots across key ports from Marseille to Naples. Naples currently reports 56°F, NE winds 4 knots, and low rain probability, but expect increasing rain chances—up to 48% on Day 2 with moderate NE winds near 12 knots. Yacht and fishing captains should plan for occasional showers and reduced visibility offshore near Civitavecchia and Livorno, and monitor conditions ahead of approaching rain on Days 2 and 3. Cruise passengers should carry light rain gear and be prepared for overcast skies and occasional wet weather, particularly around coastal stops like Genoa and Split. No small craft or gale warnings at this time.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Eastern Mediterranean weather for March 15, 2026, remains calm with clear skies and light west-southwest winds near 2 knots at Athens/Piraeus, temperature steady at 54°F. Expect a gradual shift to overcast skies with light easterly winds 4-6 knots through Day 1 and Day 2, accompanied by dense fog in the early mornings around Istanbul and Izmir; reduced visibility may affect small craft navigation. By Day 3, northeast winds increase to 10 knots with a 15% chance of light rain near Rhodes and Cyprus—no advisories necessary but monitor conditions if fishing or anchoring offshore. Passengers should plan outdoor activities in Athens and Santorini early in the day before fog and overcast conditions develop; yacht captains should ensure fog navigation systems are operational near coastal fog-prone ports like Izmir and Corfu.
